---
title: "Research"
description: "Our areas of focus, working papers, and published work."
---

## Research Areas

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🌏 Development Economics
We study the determinants of long-run growth, poverty, and inequality in developing countries.
Using nationally representative surveys and administrative data, we examine how policies and
economic shocks affect the welfare of households across the income distribution.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🏛️ Public Finance & Fiscal Policy
Our team evaluates tax policy, government expenditure, and fiscal decentralisation. We assess
how public budgets shape service delivery, local governance, and regional economic
disparities in Indonesia.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🌱 Environmental & Climate Economics
We analyse the economic impacts of climate change, natural disasters, and environmental
regulation. Current projects include the distributional effects of carbon pricing and
the economics of green industrial policy in ASEAN.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 📦 International Trade & Investment
Our trade researchers investigate how tariff changes, global value chains, and foreign direct
investment affect domestic firms, workers, and household welfare in emerging economies.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 💻 Digital Economy & Financial Inclusion
Rapid digital adoption offers new opportunities for financial inclusion. We study how digital
payments, fintech, and e-commerce transform consumption, savings, and labour market
outcomes in rural and urban Indonesia.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 📈 Applied Econometrics & Methods
We develop and apply modern empirical methods — instrumental variables, regression
discontinuity, difference-in-differences, synthetic control, and machine-learning
approaches — to causal identification in economics.
:::

:::

---

## Working Papers

*Pre-publication work open for comments*

**Digital Financial Inclusion and Rural Consumption Smoothing: Evidence from Indonesian Villages**  
Krisna Gupta, Budi Santoso, Anisa Widyani · *Working Paper* · 2026  
Using a randomised rollout of mobile banking services across 1,200 Indonesian villages, we
estimate the causal effect of digital financial access on consumption volatility, precautionary
savings, and risk-sharing among low-income rural households.  
`Digital Finance` `RCT` `Rural Development`

---

**Commodity Boom, Dutch Disease, and Structural Change in Indonesia: 2000–2022**  
Arya Pratama, Siti Aminah · *Working Paper* · 2025  
This paper documents how the 2000s commodity super-cycle reshaped Indonesia's industrial
structure and investigates whether resource windfalls crowded out manufacturing via real
exchange-rate appreciation.  
`Natural Resources` `Structural Transformation` `Indonesia`

---

**Minimum Wage Hikes and Informality: A Bunching Estimator for the Indonesian Labour Market**  
Dinda Rachmania, Yudo Prabowo · *Working Paper* · 2025  
We exploit kinks in the minimum wage schedule to identify labour-demand elasticities and
assess the extent to which formal wage floors push workers into informal employment.  
`Labour Economics` `Informality` `Bunching`

---

## Published Research

*Peer-reviewed articles, book chapters, and policy reports*

**Trade Liberalisation and Household Welfare in Indonesia: Evidence from District-Level Panel Data**  
Krisna Gupta, Dinda Rachmania, Arya Pratama · *Journal of Development Economics* · 2024  
We exploit variation in tariff reductions to identify the causal effect of trade openness on
household consumption and poverty rates across 500+ Indonesian districts from 2000–2020.  
`Trade` `Indonesia` `Causal Inference`

---

**Fiscal Decentralisation, Local Governance Quality, and Public Service Delivery**  
Siti Aminah, Budi Santoso, Krisna Gupta · *World Development* · 2024  
Using a regression-discontinuity design around population thresholds that determine local
government tiers, we estimate the effect of decentralisation on health and education outcomes.  
`Fiscal Policy` `Public Finance` `RDD`

---

**Carbon Pricing in a Developing Economy: Labour Market and Distributional Effects**  
Dinda Rachmania, Yudo Prabowo · *Energy Policy* · 2023  
A CGE model calibrated to the Indonesian input-output table assesses how alternative
carbon-pricing schemes affect wages, employment, and income distribution across sectors.  
`Climate` `CGE` `Labour Economics`

---

**The Returns to Education and Skills in Indonesia: A Meta-Analysis**  
Budi Santoso, Anisa Widyani · *Asian Development Review* · 2023  
Combining 80 estimates from 30 studies, we document heterogeneous returns to schooling across
gender, region, and sector of employment, and assess publication bias using funnel-plot regression.  
`Education` `Human Capital` `Meta-analysis`

---

**Non-Tariff Barriers, Global Value Chains, and Firm-Level Productivity in ASEAN**  
Arya Pratama, Krisna Gupta · *Review of International Economics* · 2022  
`Trade` `GVC` `Firm Productivity`

---

## Data & Replication

*We are committed to open and reproducible research*

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 💾 Datasets
Cleaned and harmonised datasets used in our research are deposited in public
repositories for replication and secondary use.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 🖥️ Code
Replication code for our papers is available on
[GitHub](https://github.com/den-econ).
We use R, Python, and Stata.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 📋 Pre-registration
We pre-register our studies on the AEA RCT Registry and OSF to ensure
transparency and reduce publication bias.
:::

:::
